key_insight: HCC-STAR is a clinician-aligned LLM that turns HCC EMR narratives into risk stratification, guideline-consistent treatment ranking, and survival estimates.
clinical_relevance: It may support faster, more consistent treatment decisions in hepatocellular carcinoma by combining staging, evidence-based reasoning, and personalized prognosis.
method: Curated ~30,000 HCC cases from SEER and expanded them into EMR-style narratives with clinician-validated prompt-based augmentation.
Built a knowledge-aligned reasoning framework with step-verifiable composite rewards to improve clinical reasoning beyond guideline memorization.
Tested on a multi-center cohort of 6,668 patients from 12 hospitals in China and compared against guidelines and leading AI models.
evidence_signal: In the abstract, HCC-STAR outperformed guidelines and competitive models in treatment recommendation and risk stratification, with clinician reviewers rating its justifications as trustworthy.
watch_item: The results come from retrospective and hypothetical survival analyses, so prospective validation and careful workflow integration are still needed before real-world use.
paper_title: Towards Precision Therapy in Hepatocellular Carcinoma: A Clinical-Reasoning LLM for Risk Stratification and Treatment Guidance
